Elara heads for the heavy steel door. She doesn't wait for an answer.

CUT TO:
working.68:
EXT. METROPOLIS - NIGHT

The analog broadcast tower. Steel cables vibrate. A low, electric thrum rattles the air. 

High above the smog, the transmitter pulses. Blue light bleeds into the clouds.

EXT. CITY STREETS - CONTINUOUS

Giant LED billboards—usually selling "Perfect Sleep"—flicker. Static tears the image. 

JULIAN VANCE appears on screen. Not the polished CEO. This is raw, hidden-camera footage. He nods. A suppressed pistol fires. A whistleblower falls. 

A COURIER stops his bike. He stares up. His eyes glow with the neural feed. He gasps, clutching his temples. 

The crowd halts. A thousand faces tilted toward the sky. 

A WOMAN rips the Somnus patch from her neck. She screams. It starts a fire.

INT. SOMNUS HQ - OFFICE - CONTINUOUS

VANCE stands by his analog garden. He prunes a white orchid with silver shears. 

On the wall, forty monitors show the same thing: his own face, committing murder. 

Vance puts the shears down. He takes a silk handkerchief from his pocket. He wipes his palms. Once. Twice. 

The heavy oak door shudders. The sound of boots on marble. 

VANCE
(to himself)
The architecture was sound. The users... the users are simply too volatile.

The glass wall behind him shatters. FEDERAL AGENTS swarm in. Red and blue strobes paint the room. 

Vance doesn't look at them. He realigns a fountain pen on his desk so it sits perfectly parallel to the edge.

EXT. PIRATE RADIO TOWER - ROOFTOP - CONTINUOUS

KNOX hunches over the console. Her fingers fly across the keys. She wraps her arms around her ribs, squeezing tight.

KNOX
Eighty-nine percent saturation. The firewalls are ash. 

JAX sits on a crate, tapping a frantic binary rhythm on his thigh. He looks at the city below. It’s glowing with a new, jagged light.

JAX
You did it. You actually burned the sky. 

KNOX
History is just a series of corrected data points.

ELARA leans against the parapet. She’s pale. Blood crusts under her nose. 

She isn't looking at the screens. She’s looking at a crumpled hospital bill in her hand. 

ELARA
How long until the servers stay down?

JAX
They're dead, Elara. The whole network is flatlining. 

Elara starts to hum. A soft, low lullaby. It’s thin and brittle.

EXT. PLAZA - CONTINUOUS

SILAS stands in the middle of a riot. Protesters sprint past him, hurling bricks through corporate glass. 

Silas doesn't move. His left eye twitches. His hands shake. 

The "Perfect Clarity" in his head is gone. A thousand voices—old, forgotten voices—flood back in. 

SILAS
Three... forty-two... AM. The colors are too loud.

He looks at his hands. They're covered in soot. He looks at a nearby wall. 

He pulls a charcoal stick from a discarded bag. He begins to draw a delicate, sweeping curve on the concrete.

EXT. METROPOLIS - NIGHT

The Somnus logo atop the tallest skyscraper flickers. The 'S' dies. Then the rest. 

The city plunges into darkness, lit only by the fires of the people waking up.

FADE OUT.
working.69:
INT. SUNLIT BEDROOM - DAY

Silence. Not the ringing void of a vacuum, but the heavy, velvet quiet of a soundproofed sanctuary. 

Dust motes drift through a column of thick, amber light. They move with an impossible slowness, dancing over a floor of polished wide-plank oak.

ELARA (24) sits on the edge of a bed. The mattress is a mountain of white linen and goose down. She looks small. Fragile.

Her boots—scuffed, salt-stained, and caked with the grey grime of the lower sectors—rest on a hand-woven silk rug. They look like an insult to the room.

Elara remains perfectly still. Her head is cocked to the side. She is listening.

She waits for the ping. That sharp, electronic needle that usually pricks the base of her skull every six seconds to signal a data packet arrival.

Nothing.

She waits for the low-frequency hum of the Somnus server-link. The sound of her own brain being leased by the millisecond. 

Only the sound of her own breathing. Slow. Shallow.

She lifts a datapad from the nightstand. The glass is flawless. No spiderweb cracks. No oily thumbprints. 

She taps the screen. The brightness doesn't hurt her eyes.

ON THE SCREEN:

A banking interface. Clean. Minimalist. 

TOTAL BALANCE: $428,000.00

Below the number, a scrolling list of transactions. All marked in green.

ST. JUDE’S INTENSIVE CARE - PAID IN FULL.
PROSTHETIC LUNG LEASE - OWNERSHIP TRANSFERRED.
ARREARS CLEARANCE - COMPLETE.

Elara stares at the word 'COMPLETE'. She traces the letters with her thumb. 

She swipes the screen. A new window opens. A high-definition video feed. 

In a room filled with soft blue light, her MOTHER sleeps. Not the grey, skeletal woman from the tenements. Her skin looks hydrated. Plump. A high-end ventilator hums with the precision of a Swiss watch.

A NURSE enters the frame, tucking a thermal blanket around the older woman’s shoulders. The nurse looks at the camera and offers a small, professional nod.

Elara’s hand drops. The datapad thuds softly onto the duvet. 

She looks at her wrist. The digital watch she’s worn since she was sixteen. 

09:42 AM.

Usually, this is the peak. The hour where the corporate servers demand the most bandwidth. The hour where her vision flickers with ghost-images of spreadsheets and encrypted code.

The sunlight on the wall stays steady. The shadows don't vibrate.

She reaches up, her fingers trembling as they find the neural port behind her ear. The plastic housing is cool to the touch. The status LED, which has pulsed a frantic, bleeding red for months, is dark.

She is offline.

The tension that has held her spine rigid for years begins to dissolve. It’s a physical collapse, starting at her jaw and working its way down to her leaden limbs. 

Her shoulders drop. Her chest expands, taking in a lungful of air that doesn't taste like ozone or exhaust.

She swings her legs up onto the bed. She doesn't even take off the boots. 

She sinks into the pillows. They swallow her.

Elara stares at the ceiling. The plaster is a perfect, unblemished white. 

A sob hitches in her throat—sharp and jagged—but she doesn't let it out. She doesn't have to cry. She doesn't have to bargain. She doesn't have to do anything.

Her eyelids grow heavy. The gold light of the room turns to a soft, hazy orange behind her lids.

She begins to hum. It’s a low, vibrating sound in her chest. A childhood lullaby, the notes slightly off-key but steady. 

The humming slows. Trails off into a sigh.

Her fingers, curled into the bedsheets, slowly relax. They go limp.

The world narrows down to the warmth of the sun on her skin. 

Elara closes her eyes.

She falls. Not into a digital grid or a corporate nightmare.

Just sleep. 

Deep. Dark. Empty.

FADE OUT.
working.70:
INT. PIRATE BROADCAST ROOM - NIGHT

Golden sunlight washes over ELARA (20s). She stands on a balcony overlooking a city of ivory and glass. The air smells of jasmine.

She presses a finger to a floating glass display. A progress bar hits 100%.

ELARA
(A breath)
It’s over.

The sun STUTTERS. A jagged black line slices through the sky.

The golden light PEELS away like burning paper. The ivory towers dissolve into RUST and CORRUGATED TIN.

The jasmine scent is replaced by the STENCH of ozone and stale sweat.

Elara is not on a balcony. She is slumped in a plastic chair in a room of shadows.

Her head rests against the console. A thick, dark line of blood has dried on her chin. 

Her eyes are wide. Fixed. They do not blink as the fluorescent light overhead HUMS.

The monitor in front of her displays a single, static-filled prompt: DATA PURGED. 0% UPLOADED.

SILAS (40s) steps from the corner. He moves with a mechanical, unwavering stillness. 

He stands over Elara. He reaches out and lifts her chin. Her head lolls to the side, lifeless.

Silas taps a small transmitter at his temple.

SILAS
The drive is scrubbed. The transmission was blocked at the source.

INTERCUT WITH:

INT. VANCE’S OFFICE - NIGHT

JULIAN VANCE (50s) stands in his carefully pruned analog garden. He uses a silver pair of shears to snip a dead leaf from a bonsai tree.

VANCE
(Soothing)
And the host? Is there any residual bandwidth?

INT. PIRATE BROADCAST ROOM - NIGHT

Silas checks the display on a handheld tablet. A flat line crawls across the screen.

SILAS
Zero activity. The neural architecture has collapsed. 

VANCE (V.O.)
A necessary optimization. We cannot allow corrupted files to compromise the global rollout.

Silas looks at the blood on Elara’s face. He pulls a white handkerchief from his suit pocket.

He does not wipe her face. Instead, he carefully cleans a spot of dust from the console’s keyboard.

SILAS
The site is secure. 

VANCE (V.O.)
Excellent. Reclaim the hardware and return. We move to the next phase in ten minutes.

Silas turns. He walks toward the exit, his footsteps echoing on the metal floor.

Elara remains frozen. A human shell trapped in the dark.

Beyond the window, the city’s massive neon grid pulses.

FADE OUT.

snapshot.title: The Somnus Drive
snapshot.logline: When a desperate gig worker leases her REM sleep to a tech conglomerate to pay for her mother's medical care, she inadvertently downloads encrypted footage of a corporate assassination and must survive a relentless hitman to expose the truth.

synopsis.setup: In a dystopian metropolis, a desperate gig worker named Elara struggles to afford life-saving medication for her mother. She signs a contract with the Somnus Corporation to lease her REM sleep, allowing her brain to be used as idle server space via a neural implant. However, the lucrative arrangement quickly sours as her sleep is hijacked by hyper-realistic nightmares of a corporate execution.
synopsis.escalation: Elara discovers the nightmares are actually encrypted video files of a murder committed by Somnus CEO Julian Vance. With the help of Jax, an underground cyber-tech, she learns the data is hardwired to her vitals; if she falls asleep, a corporate failsafe will permanently overwrite her consciousness. Hunted by the relentless enforcer Silas, Elara must endure a lethal marathon of sleep deprivation to reach a pirate broadcast tower.
synopsis.resolution: Elara reaches the tower to upload the evidence while battling Vance's avatar within a digital mindscape. Despite the physiological collapse brought on by exhaustion, she attempts to broadcast the truth to the city. The current draft concludes with unresolved, contradictory endings where Elara either survives in luxury or dies at the terminal, leaving the ultimate fate of the corporation and the protagonist unclear.

red_flags: Repetitive micro-expressions used as emotional shorthand, specifically Elara humming a 'childhood lullaby' in nearly every stressful scene.
Mechanical character tics repeated verbatim across different characters, such as both Jax and Silas tapping a 'binary rhythm' against their thighs.
Novelistic, unfilmable action lines describing interior states, such as 'He is a statue of corporate intent' and 'The silence is a warm, velvet coat.'
Severe structural anomalies including multiple redundant versions of the same scenes and two completely contradictory endings presented back-to-back.

strengths.headline: High-Concept Hook
strengths.detail: The premise of monetizing REM sleep as corporate server space is a brilliant, elevated sci-fi concept that effectively physicalizes gig-economy exploitation and provides a unique ticking clock.

strengths.headline: Clear Protagonist Motivation
strengths.detail: Elara's drive to save her mother grounds the abstract sci-fi elements in a relatable, emotional reality, making her initial decision to undergo the neural surgery feel earned and desperate.

strengths.headline: Visceral Narrative Engine
strengths.detail: The revelation that falling asleep will result in a permanent mind-wipe turns a basic biological necessity into the primary antagonist, creating high-stakes tension throughout the second act.

weaknesses.headline: Massive Structural Redundancies
weaknesses.detail: The draft is littered with duplicate scenes and contradictory endings. This lack of narrative commitment undermines the thematic resolution and suggests a failure to consolidate different drafting iterations.

weaknesses.headline: Flat, Archetypal Antagonists
weaknesses.detail: Julian Vance and Silas are cartoonish in their villainy, relying on tired cyberpunk cliches like pruning bonsai trees or acting as robotic, emotionless 'cleaners' without human depth.

weaknesses.headline: On-the-Nose Dialogue
weaknesses.detail: Characters frequently state the subtext and themes out loud. Dialogue like 'Truth is a variable. Efficiency is a constant' feels like a corporate manifesto rather than natural conversation.

weaknesses.headline: Physical Toll Disconnect
weaknesses.detail: While the script emphasizes Elara's extreme sleep deprivation, she continues to perform incredible feats of physical agility in Act III, creating a logic gap between her condition and her capabilities.

weaknesses.headline: Underdeveloped Supporting Arcs
weaknesses.detail: Jax functions primarily as an exposition-delivery device. His transition from a cynical hermit to a martyr for Elara's cause feels unearned and driven by plot necessity rather than character growth.

macro_todo.task: Consolidate the timeline by deleting all duplicate scenes and committing to one definitive ending.
Streamline Act II chase sequences by combining the subway and industrial yard scenes into one escalating set-piece to reduce repetition.
Humanize the antagonists by giving Silas and Vance motivations or flaws beyond 'corporate efficiency' and removing the bonsai tree cliche.
Ground the physical stakes of Elara's exhaustion in Act III, ensuring her sleep deprivation hinders her agency and leads to critical mistakes.
Flesh out Jax's character arc to provide a stronger personal reason for his sacrifice against Somnus.
Clarify the technical rules of the Somnus neural port and the 'failsafe' earlier in Act I to establish clear stakes for the audience.

micro_todo.task: Remove the repetitive 'childhood lullaby' humming tic and find varied visual ways to show Elara's anxiety.
Fix the 'binary tapping' error by assigning the tic to only one character or removing it entirely.
Trim unfilmable action lines and replace novelistic descriptions with objective, shootable visual cues.
Rewrite Julian Vance's dialogue in the mindscape to sound less like a cartoon villain and more like a desperate man protecting his empire.
Differentiate Jax's dialogue from Elara's to ensure they have distinct voices rather than sharing a single cynical cadence.
Scrub repetitive dialogue loops, such as Elara's constant 'How much?' and 'How long?' questions.
Remove the on-screen bank balance visual crutch and trust the performance to convey Elara's financial desperation.

recommendation.justification: The core concept of monetizing REM sleep is a fantastic, highly marketable sci-fi hook that offers a timely critique of the gig economy. While the script currently suffers from significant structural redundancies and repetitive AI-generated prose, the strength of the central premise and the visceral ticking clock make it a strong candidate for a focused rewrite.

snapshot.title: The Somnus Drive
snapshot.logline: When a desperate gig worker rents out her REM sleep as cloud storage for a tech conglomerate, she begins experiencing hyper-realistic nightmares that reveal a massive corporate conspiracy, forcing her to stay awake long enough to expose the truth before her mind is wiped.

synopsis.setup: Elara, a desperate gig worker in a dystopian metropolis, struggles to pay her mother's exorbitant medical bills. To make ends meet, she leases her REM sleep to the Somnus corporation, allowing them to use her brain as idle server space. The arrangement seems like a dark but necessary lifeline until her neural port downloads a massive, encrypted file that forces her to witness a hyper-realistic nightmare of a corporate assassination.
synopsis.escalation: Realizing the nightmare is actual footage of Somnus CEO Julian Vance murdering his co-founder, Elara becomes a walking target. She teams up with Jax, a cynical underground cyber-doc, to extract the data, but the Somnus failsafe prevents her from sleeping without triggering a lethal data-wipe. Hunted relentlessly by Silas, a mechanized corporate hitman, Elara must push her failing, sleep-deprived body to the absolute limit as they navigate the city's treacherous underbelly.
synopsis.resolution: In a desperate final bid, Elara and Jax breach a pirate broadcast tower to upload the murder footage to the global grid. As Silas closes in and Vance attempts to digitally lobotomize her from within the Somnus mindscape, Elara uses her own traumatic memories to overload the corporate servers. The script currently fractures into alternate endings, ranging from a successful upload and recovery to a bleak conclusion where Elara is left brain-dead, leaving her ultimate fate unresolved.

red_flags: Repetitive micro-expressions (humming a lullaby, binary tapping) used as emotional shorthand across multiple scenes.
Superlative-heavy, novelistic action lines describing unfilmable sensory details or interior states.
Supporting characters functioning as precision-tooled archetypes with no human contradictions or observed humanity.
On-the-nose dialogue where characters explicitly state their thematic purpose or thesis without subtext.
Inclusion of multiple, conflicting endings appended to the end of the document.

strengths.headline: High-Concept Hook
strengths.detail: The core concept of renting out REM sleep for corporate server space is a brilliant, highly marketable sci-fi premise that perfectly physicalizes the theme of gig-economy exploitation.

strengths.headline: Built-In Ticking Clock
strengths.detail: The mechanism of Elara needing to stay awake to prevent the data from overwriting her brain adds excellent built-in tension to the Act II and Act III chase sequences.

strengths.headline: Cynical Foil (Jax)
strengths.detail: Jax brings a much-needed cynical energy and sharp, world-weary dialogue that contrasts well with Elara's stoicism and desperation.

strengths.headline: Grounded Protagonist Motivation
strengths.detail: Elara's motivation is crystal clear and relatable; the crushing weight of her mother's medical debt gives her a strong, sympathetic engine that drives the plot.

weaknesses.headline: Structural Bloat and Repetition
weaknesses.detail: The script suffers from repetitive action sequences in Act II, with the protagonists running from Silas through various generic settings without escalating the core narrative or changing the dynamic.

weaknesses.headline: Uncommitted Resolution
weaknesses.detail: The inclusion of multiple, contradictory endings shows a lack of authorial commitment to Elara's thematic journey and undermines the narrative's impact.

weaknesses.headline: On-The-Nose Dialogue
weaknesses.detail: Characters frequently state their exact thematic purpose or corporate philosophy directly, leaving no room for subtext and making the antagonist feel like a cartoon villain.

weaknesses.headline: Robotic Antagonist
weaknesses.detail: Silas functions solely as an unstoppable terminator archetype with no human contradictions, making his presence feel more like a mechanical obstacle than a compelling character.

weaknesses.headline: Logic of Physical Performance
weaknesses.detail: The ticking clock of sleep deprivation is undermined by Elara's ability to function at peak physical capacity after 70+ hours awake, making her survival feel unearned.

macro_todo.task: Commit to a single, definitive ending that resolves Elara's arc and thematic journey, removing all alternate conclusions.
Consolidate the Act II chase sequences to reduce repetitive encounters with Silas and focus on escalating the stakes.
Deepen Silas and Vance beyond archetypes by giving them humanizing contradictions, flaws, or grounded motivations.
Ground Elara's physical deterioration by establishing concrete, escalating consequences for her sleep deprivation that affect her tactical choices.
Ensure the climax remains grounded in the physical world so the real-world stakes are not overshadowed by the digital mindscape battle.

micro_todo.task: Remove the repetitive micro-expression crutches, specifically the constant lullaby humming and binary tapping.
Rewrite novelistic action lines into visual, shootable cues, removing descriptions of unfilmable internal states.
Add subtext to Vance's dialogue so he sounds like a tech CEO who believes he is saving the world rather than a manifesto generator.
Reduce the frequency of Elara checking her bank balance app to avoid redundant UI reminders of her motivation.
Cut the excessive use of 'CUT TO:' and remove any remaining formatting artifacts like '[SCENE DELETED]' tags.

recommendation.justification: The script boasts a highly marketable, elevated sci-fi premise with a strong ticking-clock mechanic and a sympathetic lead. However, the execution is currently bogged down by repetitive action beats, algorithmic prose tics, and a lack of structural commitment to a single ending, requiring a rigorous rewrite to unlock its commercial potential.

working.1:
EXT. UPPER SPIRES - NIGHT

Acid rain sizzles against the rusted fairing of a battered hover-bike. The droplets eat through the grime, leaving tiny, smoking pockmarks.

ELARA (24) hunches over the handlebars. Her skin is the color of wet parchment. Her eyes are rimmed with the angry red of a seventy-hour wake cycle.

She presses two fingers against her carotid artery. Her lips move in a silent, rhythmic count.

One. Two. Three.

A jagged neon spire pierces the clouds. Clean. Silent.

Elara pulls up to a gleaming chrome delivery port. She pulls a synthetic protein pack from her courier bag. The vacuum-sealed plastic is pristine.

She slides the pack into the chute. A SENSOR sweeps over her retinas.

AUTOMATED VOICE
Delivery confirmed.


Elara taps a cracked interface on her wrist.

ELARA
How much?


The screen flickers. *+12.00 CREDITS. TOTAL BALANCE: 84.50.*

ELARA
(to herself)
Not enough.


She wipes the acid-sting from her forehead. Her hand trembles. Digging her nails into the skin until it welts.

She kicks the bike into gear. The engine COUGHS a plume of black smoke, tainting the sterile air of the spire.

She banks the bike over the edge.

EXT. LOWER SECTORS - CONTINUOUS

Elara drops.

The silence of the spires is replaced by a roar of sound. Heavy bass from street-level clubs. The shriek of mag-lev trains. The wet slap of tires on flooded asphalt.

The air thickens with the smell of ozone and cheap cooking oil.

Elara’s head lolls toward her chest. Her eyelids flutter.

A heavy-duty transport truck BLARES its horn, missing her by inches.

Elara jerks upright. She GASPS, her lungs burning in the smog.

She checks her wrist display. A notification blips: *SOMNUS UPLOAD PENDING. CONNECT TO RECHARGE.*

She ignores the alert. She twists the throttle, weaving through a canyon of flickering neon signs and tangled power lines.

She’s not going home. She’s looking for the next drop.

CUT TO:
working.2:
INT. APARTMENT COMPLEX - HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS

Elara shoulders a rusted security door. It shrieks against the concrete, resisting until she heaves her full weight into the frame.

She stumbles into the corridor. Overhead, a fluorescent tube flickers in a jagged, rhythmic seizure.

Shadows stretch and snap against the peeling wallpaper.

Elara drags her boots across the stained carpet. 

She reaches Unit 4B and digs into her pocket. Her hand trembles.

Fingers brush past a crumpled stack of medical bills before she pulls out a plastic keycard.

She leans her forehead against the cool metal of the door frame. Her eyes are bloodshot.

The keycard reader chirps. A dull, mechanical click.

CUT TO:
working.3:
INT. ELARA'S APARTMENT - KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS

Elara drops her canvas tool bag. The heavy clatter of wrenches and scavenged copper pipes hits the linoleum.

The room is dark, save for a pulsing crimson light on the scarred wooden table.

A holographic mailer floats above a cracked tablet. The text is bold, sterile.

FINAL NOTICE: TREATMENT CESSATION.

Elara pulls a chair out. The screech of wood on tile is the only sound. She stares at the red glow.

ARREARS: $4,200.00.
DISCONNECT DATE: IMMEDIATE.

She doesn't blink. Her thumb traces the edge of the table, finding a deep gouge in the wood.

ELARA
How much.

She taps her wrist. A flickering interface projects onto her forearm. 

Current Balance: $14.12.
Next Somnus Payout: 06:00.

She stands and moves to the window. The glass is spider-webbed.

Outside, the Somnus Corporate Spire pierces the smog. It glows with a soft, clean blue.

Elara rubs her temples. Her fingers catch on the small, silver port behind her ear. The skin around it is raw.

ELARA
Eight hours.

She checks the bank app one last time. 

ELARA
I need twelve.

She turns away from the view. The red light of the notice paints her face.
working.4:
INT. ELARA'S APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT

The air smells of ozone and reheated soy. A single lamp hums, casting a flickering yellow glow over a pile of discarded circuit boards in the corner.

SILVIA (60s) sits in a frayed armchair, her frame swallowed by the shadows. Her knitting needles click with a frantic, metallic speed. A half-finished sleeve of grey wool jumps in her lap, but it cannot hide the tremor in her hands. The needles slip, clattering against each other.

ELARA (20s) enters. She moves like her joints are fused. Her eyes are rimmed with red, the skin beneath them bruised purple. 

She drops a plastic bag of synth-proteins on the scarred table.

ELARA
Eat the green ones first. They expire Tuesday.

Silvia doesn't look up. The needles continue their blur. She grips the wood until her knuckles turn white.

SILVIA
I heard you. This morning.

ELARA
I don't remember it.

Elara reaches for Silvia’s forehead. Her touch is brief, a flicker of tenderness. Silvia catches Elara’s hand. Her skin is parchment-thin, vibrating with a neurological misfire.

SILVIA
It’s a lease, Elara. That’s all it is.

ELARA
(pulling away)
It’s a mortgage. For your meds.

Silvia pulls Elara closer. Her voice is a dry, jagged whisper.

SILVIA
Don’t let them rent the space behind your eyes. Once they map the floorplan, they never move out.

Elara’s spine locks. She pulls her arm back, her fingers flying to her wrist-mounted interface. She scrolls, the blue light of the screen washing out her face.

ELARA
I have three courier shifts starting at 0400. That’s forty credits. If I take the double-black route through the docks, it’s a twenty percent hazard bonus.

SILVIA
Elara. Look at me.

ELARA
The next tier of your neuro-inhibitors is six thousand credits. If I maintain ninety percent sleep-density for the month, we hit the goal by Friday.

She taps the screen. Hard.

ELARA (CONT'D)
Ten days. Then we’re clear.

SILVIA
You aren't there anymore. When you close your eyes, you’re just... hardware.

Elara turns toward the kitchenette. She doesn't look back.

ELARA
Hardware doesn't starve.

She grabs a glass.

ELARA (CONT'D)
Go to bed, Ma. I need the room quiet for the upload.

Silvia stares at the grey wool. The needles start again. Fast. Faster.

CUT TO:
working.5:
INT. ELARA'S BEDROOM - NIGHT

Shadows swallow the corners of the room. ELARA (24) sits cross-legged on a mattress that is more springs than foam. The only light comes from a datapad, its screen shattered into a crystalline web.

Her thumb dances across the glass. Fast. Precise. 

ON THE SCREEN: A digital ledger. Red numbers bleed across the display.

She drags a tiny cluster of credits from a folder labeled RENT to one labeled MED-DEBT.

AUTOMATED VOICE
Transfer incomplete. Minimum payment for Life-Support Tier 4 is 800 credits. Current balance: 142.

ELARA
Override. Allocate from "Nutrition."

AUTOMATED VOICE
Nutrition balance is zero, Elara.

She stares at the number. 142. It doesn't blink. It doesn't grow.

She opens her work portal. A calendar of double shifts. She tries to drag a 4:00 AM block into her current schedule.

AUTOMATED VOICE
Error. Mandatory four-hour biological reset required by Labor Code 402.

Elara’s jaw tightens. She tries again. Faster. The screen flickers under the pressure of her thumb.

ELARA
How much for a double-overtime waiver?

AUTOMATED VOICE
Waivers are currently unavailable for Tier 3 contractors.

A soft, melodic hum vibrates in her throat—a haunting, rhythmic lullaby. 

She hammers her fist into the console. The screen goes black. 

Silence fills the room. Elara looks at her reflection in the dead glass. Pale. Hollowed out. 

She reaches into her pocket and pulls out a crumpled business card: SOMNUS.

She checks her watch. 02:14 AM.

ELARA
How long to sync?

She isn't asking the room. She's asking the card. 

CUT TO:
working.6:
INT. ELARA'S BEDROOM - NIGHT

The room is a cramped box of peeling wallpaper and exposed wiring. 

ELARA (20s) sits on the edge of a sagging mattress. She stares at a hand-held DATAPAD. A digital ledger bleeds red. 

TOTAL DUE: $4,200.00.

She taps the screen. It flickers, then dies. 

ELARA
Not now.

She shakes the device. A burst of light erupts from the glass.

A HOLOGRAPHIC SPOKESMAN—suave, radiating a calm, curated warmth—hovers in the center of the room. He stands in a digital field of swaying lavender.

SPOKESMAN
Your mind is untapped real estate. Why let it go to waste?

Elara squints against the artificial glare. She reaches for the power button, but stops.

SPOKESMAN
Somnus turns your REM cycles into stability. No labor. No stress. Just optimization.

The hologram displays a ticker. The numbers climb with hypnotic speed. 

$250 per night. $1,750 per week.

Elara’s gaze drifts to the nightstand. A stack of orange "FINAL NOTICE" envelopes weighs down a bottle of generic heart medication.

She looks back at the glowing ticker. Her thumb trembles over a pulsing green ACCEPT icon.

SPOKESMAN
Unlock your bandwidth. Help us build the future while you rest.

Elara’s breath hitches. She remembers a face from the docks—a man with hollow eyes and a permanent stutter. "They don't just use the space," he’d whispered. "They leave footprints."

She rubs the base of her skull. A dull ache thumps behind her eyes.

ELARA
How long is the contract?

The Spokesman doesn't break his loop. He just gestures to the pulse of the green button.

Elara pulls her hand back, curling her fingers into a fist. She taps her bank app instead.

BALANCE: $12.04.

She closes her eyes. The lavender light of the ad bleeds through her eyelids, turning her world a sickly, synthetic violet.

CUT TO:
working.7:
INT. ELARA'S APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT

The low hum of a struggling window A/C unit fills the cramped space. Shadows stretch across peeling wallpaper.

A ceramic mug SHATTERS.

ELARA (24) lunges from the kitchen, skidding on the linoleum. 

SILVIA (50s) is on the floor. Her body is a knotted wire of tension, back arching, heels drumming a frantic, hollow rhythm against the floorboards. 

ELARA
Mom!

Elara hits the floor. She grabs a throw pillow, wedging it beneath Silvia’s head just before it cracks against the baseboard. 

Silvia’s eyes are wide, unseeing, fixed on the flickering ceiling light. A thin, silver string of saliva leaks from her clenched jaw.

ELARA
Look at me. Silvia, look at me.

Elara’s hands are steady, but her breath comes in jagged pulls. She reaches for a plastic pill bottle on the coffee table. 

Empty. 

She grabs her phone. The screen is cracked. A banking app glows: $12.48.

SILVIA
(a strangled rasp)
...dark...

ELARA
Don't talk. Don't try to talk.

Silvia’s hand spasms, catching Elara’s wrist with surprising, bruising strength. The older woman’s fingernails dig into Elara’s skin. 

Elara doesn't flinch. She begins to hum. A soft, low-frequency lullaby. It’s a haunting, mechanical sound.

She swipes the phone screen. A new notification sits over the empty bank balance: SOMNUS - ENROLLMENT PENDING.

ELARA
How long for the first deposit?

She’s looking at the ceiling, her voice flat, transactional.

ELARA
I need the Tier 1 override. Now.

Silvia’s tremors intensify. A wet, rattling sound starts in her chest. 

Elara taps the Somnus app. A digital contract scrolls past at a dizzying speed. 

ELARA
Fine. Take it.

She presses her thumb to the scanner. A red ring pulses on the screen. 

TEXT ON SCREEN: NEURAL LINK ESTABLISHED. MAPPING BIOLOGICAL SERVER SPACE...

Elara winces. Her left eye leaks a single tear, but her expression remains a stoic mask. She pulls Silvia closer, pinning her mother’s thrashing limbs against her own chest.

ELARA
(whispering)
Six hours. That’s all. Six hours and we buy the vials.

The phone chimes. A cold, synthetic blue light washes over them both.

TEXT ON SCREEN: HOSTING ACTIVE. EARNINGS: $0.00/HR (CALIBRATING).

Silvia’s body goes limp. The violent energy vanishes, leaving her a hollow weight in Elara’s arms. 

Elara checks the pulse. Thready. Weak. But the seizing has stopped.

Elara stares at the "Calibrating" bar on her phone. She doesn't move. She doesn't cry. She just watches the numbers.

CUT TO:
working.8:
EXT. SOMNUS CLINIC - DAY

A rusted hover-bike screeches to a halt, kicking up a cloud of oily grit against the curb.

ELARA (24) kills the engine. The machine rattles, a dying cough in the silence. She wipes a smear of grease from her jaw with a grimy sleeve.

She looks up.

The Somnus Clinic. A towering slab of white composite and reflective glass. It stands like a clean tooth in a mouth of rot. 

Elara pulls back her cuff. A flickering holographic display on her wrist reads: $14.12. 

She checks her watch. 08:59.

She marches toward the entrance. Her heavy, salt-stained boots thud against the acid-etched pavement. 

The glass doors slide open with a pressurized hiss. A gust of climate-controlled, scentless air hits her. 

Elara hesitates, then steps over the threshold.

CUT TO:
working.9:
INT. SOMNUS CLINIC - WAITING ROOM - DAY

White walls. White floor. White ceiling. The light doesn't come from a fixture; the room itself glows with a flat, shadowless intensity.

ELARA (20s) sits on a bench that is little more than a molded curve of frosted glass. She looks small. Brittle. 

She taps her thigh. Fast. A rhythmic twitch.

She pulls a cracked smartphone from her pocket. The screen flickers. 

BANK INTERFACE: $14.12. 

A red notification banner crawls across the top: ST. JUDE’S CARE FACILITY - URGENT - 48 HOURS UNTIL DISCHARGE.

Elara locks the phone. Her reflection in the black glass is pale. 

A soft chime, like a silver spoon hitting crystal, echoes through the room.

A HOLOGRAPHIC INTERFACE blooms in the air before her. Thousands of lines of glowing cerulean text spiral into a dense, rotating cylinder.

VIRTUAL ATTENDANT (V.O.)
(soothing, melodic)
The Somnus network thrives on transparency. Please review the Tier 3 Data-Hosting Addendum.

Elara doesn't read. Her eyes track the blur of "Sub-Neural Partitioning" and "Cognitive Liability Waivers." 

ELARA
How long?

VIRTUAL ATTENDANT (V.O.)
The onboarding process takes approximately six minutes.

ELARA
Not the setup. The transfer. How long until the credits hit the escrow account?

VIRTUAL ATTENDANT (V.O.)
Upon biometric confirmation, the first cycle’s compensation will be available for immediate withdrawal. Your mind is a valuable asset, Elara. We treat it as such.

Elara stares at the word "IRREVERSIBLE" as it drifts past her nose in 12-point font.

She begins to hum. A low, breathy melody. "Twinkle Twinkle Little Star," but the tempo is off. It’s jagged. 

She reaches out. Her fingers hover over the blue light. The air around the hologram smells of ionized dust.

VIRTUAL ATTENDANT (V.O.)
By signing, you agree to allocate sixty percent of your REM cycle to the Somnus global server array. Do you understand the terms?

ELARA
(a whisper)
I understand the price.

She presses her thumb into the center of the rotating text. 

The blue light turns a deep, bruised violet. The hologram collapses into a single point of light, then vanishes.

A sharp, electronic chirp sounds from her pocket. 

Elara pulls out her phone. 

NOTIFICATION: Deposit Confirmed. $8,500.00.

Her thumb is still glowing with a faint, residual ultraviolet stain. She rubs it against her jeans, but the mark stays.

The far wall slides open. A corridor of even brighter white stretches into infinity.

ELARA
(to herself)
Next.

She stands, her knees locking for a second, then walks into the light.

CUT TO:
working.10:
INT. SOMNUS CLINIC - SURGICAL THEATER - LATER

The air tastes like a lightning strike. Ozone and antiseptic. 

ELARA (24) stands in the center of the room. She wears a thin paper gown. Her skin is pebbled with gooseflesh. 

A TECHNICIAN in white scrubs gestures to a high-back chair. It is a bleached, upright coffin of plastic and chrome. 

TECHNICIAN
Position your head in the cradle. Chin up.

Elara doesn't move. She stares at the heavy leather restraints. 

ELARA
The signing bonus. It clears once I’m tagged?

TECHNICIAN
Upon successful integration.

ELARA
How long?

TECHNICIAN
Standard latency. Ten minutes.

Elara climbs into the chair. The leather is freezing. The Technician pulls the straps across her wrists. Click. Her ankles. Click. 

He moves behind her. A metal brace clamps around her forehead, forcing her eyes wide. 

TECHNICIAN (CONT'D)
Try to keep your heart rate steady. It helps the calibration.

ELARA
Just do it. 

The Technician taps a glass pane. From the ceiling, a hydraulic arm unfolds. It is a multi-jointed limb of chrome and fiber-optics. 

A ruby laser dot appears on the wall. It tracks across the floor, climbs the chair, and settles on the soft indentation at the base of Elara's skull. 

The machine hisses. A cooling fan spins up to a high-pitched whine. 

Elara’s knuckles turn the color of bone against the armrests. She stares at a fixed point on the far wall—a small digital print of a sunset. 

The machine shifts. The drill bit, a needle of diamond-tipped steel, rotates. 

Elara’s breath hitches. She begins to hum. A low, vibrating melody. A childhood lullaby. 

The drill touches skin. 

The sound changes from a whine to a wet, grinding chug. 

Elara’s spine arches. Her eyes roll back, catching only the glare of the surgical LEDs. 

TECHNICIAN
Seating the port. Hold still.

The hum becomes a strained moan, muffled by gritted teeth. The drill bites deeper, vibrating through her jaw, her teeth, her very thoughts. 

A sharp crack echoes in the small room. Bone giving way to hardware. 

Elara’s body goes rigid. Then, a total, terrifying slackness. 

The red laser turns green. 

TECHNICIAN (CONT'D)
Integration complete.

Elara stares at the ceiling. She tries to lift her hand, but it is a dead weight. She tries to speak, but her tongue is a lead slug in her mouth. 

The Technician unclips the forehead brace. Her head lolls to the side. 

TECHNICIAN (CONT'D)
The numbness is a side effect of the initial handshake. Your nervous system is adjusting to the new architecture. 

He checks a tablet. 

TECHNICIAN (CONT'D)
Bonus sent. Welcome to the network, Elara. 

She cannot feel her feet. She cannot feel the chair. She is a ghost floating in a meat suit. 

On the wall, the digital sunset flickers. For a fraction of a second, the orange sun turns into a jagged, bleeding eye. 

Elara blinks. The eye is gone. Only the sunset remains. 

The Technician starts unbuckling the straps. 

                                                                CUT TO:
working.12:
INT. ELARA'S APARTMENT - KITCHEN - MORNING

Elara's eyes snap open. They are bloodshot, fixed on a yellowed water stain on the ceiling.

At the base of her skull, a thick, fiber-optic cable HISSES. The pneumatic seal breaks. The lead slides out of her neck with a wet suction, retracting into a wall-mounted Somnus port with a mechanical whine.

Elara doesn't move. Her skin is the color of wet ash. A bead of cold sweat tracks through the grime on her temple.

She reaches for a battered datapad resting on the laminate table. 

The screen pulses. A bright, neon green notification: +4,500.00 CREDITS.

Her thumb blurs across the cracked glass. She doesn't check the math. She doesn't look at the source.

She opens a bookmarked app: SYNDICATE MEDICAL DEBT.

On the table, a physical smart-paper bill glows with a rhythmic crimson light. 

FINAL NOTICE: REPOSSESSION IMMINENT.

Elara taps the screen. 'PAY IN FULL.'

The red glow on the smart-paper stutters. The light shifts, settling into a steady blue. 

ACCOUNT CLEARED. 

The datapad slips from her fingers, clattering against a mug of cold, oily coffee. 

Elara drops her head into her hands. Her shoulders hitch as she draws a jagged, shuddering breath. 

She pulls her phone from her pocket. She doesn't call anyone. She just stares at the bank balance. 

She looks at the blue light on the table. Her hands finally stop shaking.
working.13:
EXT. LOWER SECTORS - DAY

Rain needles the asphalt. A battered hover-bike leans into a sharp turn, tires HISSING against the wet grime.

ELARA (20s) hunches over the handlebars. A stack of delivery parcels is lashed to the rear rack with frayed bungee cords.

She glances at a wrist-mounted DATAPAD.

ELARA
Three minutes.

She guns the throttle. The turbine WHINES.

She weaves between a lumbering trash-hauler and a line of rusted commuters. Her eyes are bloodshot, fixed on the neon navigation line flickering on her visor.

A HUD notification pings: SOMNUS UPLINK: STABLE. 84% BUFFERED.

Elara blinks. Her pupils dilate, swallowing the iris.

The roar of the city—the sirens, the shouting, the turbine—VANISHES.

The world becomes a vacuum.

Elara’s hands remain locked on the grips. Her bike continues to drift forward, but her gaze is vacant.

A flicker of a distorted image—a dark hallway, a flash of chrome—stutters across her retinas.

SOUND RETURNS with the force of a physical blow.

The screech of brakes. A HORN blares.

EXT. NEON INTERSECTION - DAY

Elara jerks the handlebars, nearly dumping the bike.

She is idling at a red light. The street is different. The trash-hauler is gone. The rain has slowed to a heavy mist.

She looks left, then right. The "K-Mart" sign that should be behind her is gone. She’s at the corner of 4th and Main. Three blocks south.

Her chest heaves. She taps the datapad with a trembling finger.

The clock reads 14:42.

ELARA
No.

She swipes back through the log. It was 14:22 a second ago.

Twenty minutes. Deleted.

The driver behind her leans on his horn, shouting through his cracked windshield.

Elara doesn't look back. She stares at her hands.

She forces her fingers flat against the fuel tank. She starts to hum—a low, rhythmic vibration in her throat. A childhood lullaby, soft and out of place against the city's grit.

She opens her bank app. +450 Credits (Pending).

She stares at the number until the light turns green.

ELARA
Just the buffer. It's just the buffer.

She kicks the bike into gear and guns it into the traffic.
working.14:
INT. ELARA'S BEDROOM - NIGHT

The room is a cramped inventory of survival. Stacks of unpaid medical bills compete for space with half-empty saline bags.

ELARA (24) sits on the edge of a mattress that groans under her slight frame. She taps her phone screen.

ON SCREEN: A banking app. BALANCE: $12.42. RENT OVERDUE.

She sets the phone face down. Her movements are efficient, devoid of wasted motion.

She picks up the SOMNUS CABLE. The fiber-optic cord catches the dim light, shimmering like oil on water.

Her fingers find the interface at the base of her skull. The skin around the plastic port is inflamed, a ring of angry red.

She aligns the jack. Pushes.

A metallic CLICK echoes in the quiet room.

The port ignites. A steady, cool blue glow spills across her shoulders.

Elara’s eyes slide shut. Her jaw locks.

INT. ELARA'S MIND - CONTINUOUS

The Somnus server environment has soured.

The soft, drifting pastel spheres of the previous week are gone. In their place, jagged fractals spin with a high velocity. They aren't data points; they are shards of glass.

The geometry grinds together, shedding sparks of white light that sting the darkness.

INT. ELARA'S BEDROOM - NIGHT

Elara’s heels dig into the carpet. Her body hitches, caught in a low-voltage seizure.

The blue light from the port begins to strobe. It’s a frantic, uneven flicker that refuses to sync with the steady thud of her heart.

Her breath comes in shallow, jagged hitches. 

ELARA
(to the empty room)
Six hours. Just six.

She begins to hum. A soft, melodic fragment of a lullaby. It’s a fragile sound, nearly drowned out by the high-pitched whine of the neural hardware.

She doesn't reach for the cord. She grips the bedframe, her knuckles straining against the skin, and waits for the clock to start.

CUT TO:
working.15:
EXT. UPPER SPIRES - DAY

The white marble of the grand staircase reflects the sun with a blinding, clinical intensity.

ELARA (24) climbs. Each step is a negotiation. Her heavy work boots, scuffed and gray, drag across the polished stone.

SCREE.

The sound of rubber catching on marble echoes through the quiet plaza.

She stops. Her chest heaves, but the air feels thin, like she’s breathing through a wet filter. She looks up. The top of the stairs is fifty yards away.

She shifts the courier bag on her shoulder. A synthetic protein pack slips from the side pocket. It hits the stone with a dull THUD.

Elara reaches for it. Her arm moves in a slow, jagged arc. Her hand hovers over the foil pouch, fingers twitching in a rapid, rhythmic tremor.

She squeezes her fist shut. It doesn't stop.

She sinks onto the step. Not a fall, but a slow-motion structural failure.

ELARA
(to herself)
Four minutes. Just four.

She presses two fingers hard against the side of her neck. Her pulse isn't a steady beat; it’s a frantic, uneven thrumming against her fingertips.

She shuts her eyes and aggressively rubs her temples, trying to grind the pressure out of her skull. The brain-fog isn't a cloud. It’s a weight. A thick, grey sludge filling the spaces between her thoughts.

A chime sounds from her wrist. She doesn't look at the notification. She taps the screen twice, bringing up a glowing blue interface:

CREDIT BALANCE: 142.50
PENDING SURGERY DEPOSIT: 4,500.00

The numbers blur. She blinks, forcing them into focus.

ELARA
Not today.

She grabs the protein pack. Her grip is white-knuckled, fighting the shake. She shoves it back into the bag.

She plants her palms on the marble. The stone is cold. She uses that cold to anchor her mind.

She stands. Her knees lock for a second, then hold.

She doesn't look up at the remaining stairs. She looks at her boots.

Step. Scrape. Step.

The shadow of the spire stretches long across the plaza, swallowing her as she moves upward.
working.16:
INT. ELARA'S APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM - EVENING

The air is thick with the smell of scorched ozone and cheap synth-yeast. 

SILVIA (60s) sits in a threadbare armchair. Her hands, once a blur of neurological tremors, now rest still on her lap. She threads a needle with terrifying, steady precision.

The front door rattles. ELARA (20s) stumbles in. 

Her skin is the color of wet parchment. Dark circles under her eyes look like fresh bruises. 

Silvia stands. She moves with a grace she didn't have a week ago. 

SILVIA
You’re late.

ELARA
Shift ran over. 

Elara drops her keys. They clatter on the floor. She doesn't pick them up. 

Silvia steps close. Her eyes lock on Elara’s neck. Behind the ear, a NEURAL PORT pulses with a sickly, neon-amber light. The skin around the plastic rim is raw, weeping a thin, clear fluid.

Silvia reaches out, fingers hovering near the port.

SILVIA
Elara. The light. It’s changing color. It looks--

Elara snaps her head back. She swats Silvia’s hand away. 

ELARA
Don’t touch it. It’s calibrated. 

SILVIA
You’re shaking. Let me get the antiseptic.

ELARA
I’m fine. 

Elara fumbles with her pocket, pulling out a cracked DATAPAD. Her thumbs swipe, her movements jerky and mechanical.

She thrusts the screen into Silvia’s face. 

ELARA (CONT'D)
Look at the balance. 

Silvia doesn't look at the screen. She looks at her daughter's bloodshot eyes. 

SILVIA
I don’t care about the numbers. 

ELARA
You should. The Tier 3 meds are cleared. The clinic called. You start the infusion tomorrow. 

SILVIA
At what cost? You look like a ghost. 

ELARA
It’s sixteen hundred credits, Mom. For four hours of sleep. 

Elara turns away, leaning her forehead against the cool glass of the window. 

ELARA (CONT'D)
That’s three months of breathing. 

SILVIA
I’d rather wheeze and have my daughter back. 

Elara checks her bank app again. The blue light reflects in her hollow eyes. 

ELARA
You’re staying in that chair. I’m staying in the program. 

Silence fills the room, heavier than the smog outside. Elara’s left hand begins to twitch—a rhythmic, binary tap against her thigh. 

ELARA (CONT'D)
How long until dinner? 

SILVIA
Ten minutes. 

ELARA
I’m going to lie down. Don’t wake me. 

Elara moves toward the hallway, her gait uneven. As she disappears into the dark, she hums three notes of a low, tuneless lullaby.
working.17:
INT. ELARA'S BATHROOM - NIGHT

The overhead light hums, a flickering amber pulse that makes the cracked tiles vibrate. 

ELARA (24) stands over a sink stained with rings of rust. She peels her sweat-dampened shirt down past her shoulder blades. 

She picks up a jagged shard of a hand-mirror from the ledge. She holds it up, angling the reflection against the main vanity glass to see the nape of her neck.

The Somnus port is a dull, industrial hex-socket embedded at the base of her skull. The surrounding skin is a bruised, angry violet. It weeps a thin, clear fluid that tracks a glistening line down her spine.

She touches the rim of the metal. 

A sharp, electric jolt shoots through her. She gasps, her knees buckling against the vanity. 

She stares at herself in the glass. Her eyes are bloodshot, the whites turned the color of scorched parchment. Dark, hollowed-out craters sit beneath them. 

She reaches back again. This time, her fingers hook under the edge of the metal casing. 

She pulls. 

The skin stretches taut. A wet, tearing sound fills the small room. 

Elara grinds her teeth. A low, tuneless hum vibrates in her throat—a soft, repetitive childhood lullaby. Her knuckles turn white as she prepares to rip the hardware from her bone.

Her phone pings on the edge of the porcelain. 

She freezes. Her eyes dart to the screen.

NOTIFICATION: CENTRAL HEALTHCARE - OVERDUE BALANCE: $4,200.00.

Beneath it, the Somnus app icon glows with a predatory green light. 

CURRENT SESSION EARNINGS: $142.50 (PROCESSING...)

The humming stops. 

Elara’s hand trembles, then slowly slides away from the back of her neck. The skin snaps back around the port. 

She leans over the sink, staring into the drain. She watches a single drop of infected fluid fall and vanish into the dark pipe.

She taps her phone screen. She doesn't look at a photo or a message. She looks at the bank balance. 

ELARA
(a whisper)
Two more cycles. 

She turns the cold tap. The pipes groan. She splashes the rusty water over her face, scrubbing hard until her skin is raw. 

She pulls her shirt back up, hiding the metal, the infection, and the cost. 

She checks her watch. 

CUT TO:
working.18:
INT. ELARA'S BEDROOM - NIGHT

The room is a coffin of peeling wallpaper and stacked medical crates. The only light leaks from a tablet propped on a milk crate.

ACCOUNT BALANCE: $14.12.

ELARA (24) sits on the edge of a sagging mattress. She checks her watch. 11:59 PM. 

She reaches for a heavy, shielded cable dangling from a bedside rack. The connector is scuffed, the copper teeth dulled by use. 

She finds the port at the base of her skull—a silver-rimmed divot in her hairline. She jams the cable home with a wet, metallic THUD.

ELARA
(a breathy prayer)
Eight hours. Just give me eight.

A rhythmic BEEP pulses from a monitor. 

Elara’s spine stiffens. Her eyelids flutter, then seal shut.

INT. SOMNUS INTERFACE - NIGHT (DREAMSCAPE)

A horizon of soft lavender light expands in every direction. It is silent. Weightless. 

A hairline fracture ripples through the purple sky. 

The lavender sky peels back like scorched film, curling into black ash. Underneath, a torrent of jagged, white-hot static floods the frame. 

Binary rot eats the horizon. The rhythmic beep on the monitor outside accelerates into a flatline whine.

INT. ELARA'S BEDROOM - NIGHT

Elara’s body spasms. Her heels dig into the thin sheets, dragging them loose. 

Her jaw locks. A low, trembling sound vibrates in her throat—a childhood lullaby, tuneless and terrified.

The monitor flashes a strobing crimson.

SYSTEM ERROR: BUFFER OVERFLOW.

Elara’s eyes snap open. The pupils are blown wide, reflecting the frantic, flickering white light of the crashing system. 

CUT TO:
working.19:
INT. CORPORATE BOARDROOM - NIGHT

Static flickers. Not behind the eyes, but in the air. 

The image snaps into focus. High-altitude. 80th floor. The city below is a grid of neon veins, pulsing with a rhythm that feels wrong.

ELARA (V.O.)
(a ragged whisper)
Stop. Wake up. 

She can’t. Her perspective is fixed. Locked into a height of six feet. The head doesn’t turn. The eyes don’t blink. 

The boardroom is a tomb of glass and polished obsidian. A long table reflects the moonlight like a black lake.

At the far end, a MAN (50s) scrambles backward. His bespoke suit is ruined, the silk tie frayed. He isn’t running; he’s rowing with his heels across the plush carpet.

MAN
The ledger is encrypted. You need the key. You need me.

He hits the floor-to-ceiling window. His head thumps against the glass. 

Behind him, the city lights look like a firing squad.

MAN (CONT'D)
Julian? Are you listening? The ROI... the projected growth... it doesn’t work if I’m gone.

A SHADOW moves into the periphery. SILAS. 

His left hand hangs by his thigh. The index and middle fingers tap against the fabric. One-two. Pause. One-two. A binary metronome.

VANCE (V.O.)
(filtered)
Pulse is eighty-five. Micro-tremors in the right flexor. You're drifting out of spec, Silas. Re-center.

Silas stiffens. He forces a slow, measured exhale. The tapping stops. He locks his posture into a silhouette of mathematical precision. He doesn’t breathe heavily. He doesn't hesitate.

Silas raises a hand. A suppressed PISTOL extends from the sleeve. Cold. Matte black.

MAN (CONT'D)
Wait. Wait, wait, wait--

The Man reaches out. His fingers tremble, grasping for a hand that isn’t being offered. 

MAN (CONT'D)
I have a daughter. She’s... she’s twelve. Think about the optics. Think about the--

Silas steps closer. The barrel of the gun is inches from the Man’s forehead.

ELARA (V.O.)
(straining)
Move. Please. Just move.

Elara’s perspective remains a dead-still camera. 

Silas’s thumb clicks the safety. A tiny, mechanical sound that fills the room like a thunderclap. 

His free hand twitches by his side. One-two. One-two.

MAN
Please. I can fix the data. I can rewrite the--

Silas speaks. The voice is a low, modulated hum.

SILAS
The data is already home. You’re just the noise.

A muffled CRACK.

The muzzle flash is a strobe light of white heat. 

The Man’s head snaps back. The glass behind him doesn't break. It catches the spray.

A thick, opaque spray of crimson paints the window. It’s too red. Too bright. It slides down the glass in slow, heavy ribbons.

The Man slumps. His hand slides down the obsidian table, leaving a streak of sweat and oil.

Silas turns the gun toward the camera. Toward Elara.

Through the red-stained glass, the city lights begin to blur. They stretch into long, digital lines. 

Silas’s finger tightens on the trigger.

ELARA (V.O.)
WAKE UP!

The world dissolves into a roar of white noise.

CUT TO:
working.20:
INT. ELARA'S BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS

Elara’s body bolts upright. A jagged, choked SCREAM breaks the silence.

Her hands fly to the base of her skull, fingers fumbling with the heavy coupling. She YANKS.

The fiber-optic lead tears free. Blue sparks spray across the bed. They hiss against the damp sheets, leaving tiny, scorched pinholes in the fabric.

Elara lunges backward. Her spine hits the headboard. The frame rattles against the thin wall. 

She gasps for air. Her lungs burn. Sweat coats her collarbone. She reaches back, touching the neural port. 

She hisses. The surrounding flesh is blister-hot. She presses deeper. Her fingers come away slick with a thick, grayish fluid. It smells of rotting copper.

She scrambles out of bed, knocking the nightstand. A half-empty bottle of generic painkillers clatters to the floor.

She twists her neck in front of the cracked wall mirror, catching the reflection of her nape.

The skin around the port is angry, threaded with necrotic black veins spreading outward. A slow drip of synthetic pus weeps from the socket.

Elara stares at the oily smear on her fingers. Her hand begins to shake. She presses it against her thigh, trying to pin the tremor down.

ELARA
Jax.

She grabs a dirty rag from the dresser, pressing it hard against the port. She winces, her knees buckling for a second.

She snatches her jacket from the floor, shoving her arms into the sleeves. She doesn't look back at the dead cable snaking across the rug.

CUT TO:
working.21:
EXT. SUBTERRANEAN TECH-SLUMS - DAY

Heat shimmers off floor-level exhaust vents. The air tastes of ozone and scorched copper.

ELARA (20s) descends a rusted spiral staircase. Every step is a hollow GONG that vibrates up her shins.

She stops. Grips the railing. Her knuckles are the color of bone.

She presses two fingers to her neck. Her eyes fixate on the digital clock flickering in the corner of her vision.

ELARA
One-twenty. Too high.

She rubs her temples, digging her nails into the skin.

The stairs terminate in a narrow alley choked with neon-pink rain. The water is slick with oil. It coats the pavement in a shimmering, toxic film.

Elara steps into the crowd. 

This is the city’s basement. Low ceilings. Exposed conduits weeping condensation.

A VENDOR with a chrome-plated jaw leans from a stall. He thrusts a bundle of glowing fiber-optics toward her.

VENDOR
Fresh extraction, sister. Clean signal.

Elara doesn't blink. She checks her wrist-worn bank display. The numbers are red.

ELARA
I have three minutes.

She shoves past. Her shoulder hits a man in a tattered hazard suit. She doesn't look back.

She scans the shadows. Every hooded figure is a hitman. Every flickering streetlamp is a lens.

She starts to hum. A soft, airy melody. It’s a lullaby, but the rhythm is jagged, broken by her shallow breaths.

A SECOND VENDOR, massive in a coat of thermal blankets, steps into her path. He holds a tray of neural dampeners.

She scrambles to her knees. Her breath comes in jagged, wet rasps. She clutches a water-damaged DATAPAD to her chest.

She looks up. Freezes.

SILAS (40s) stands in the center of the room. His suit is crisp. Dry. He holds a suppressed pistol leveled at Elara’s sternum.

The room hums. A wall of vintage CRT monitors flickers with green code, casting a sickly glow over his emotionless face.

Knox is gone. Only an overturned chair and a headset dangling by its cord remain.

ELARA
Where is she?

SILAS
The broadcast is terminated.

ELARA
I have the drive. I’ll give it to you. Just— how much for her?

Silas doesn't move. He doesn't look at the drive. His eyes remain fixed on the bridge of her nose.

SILAS
Zero.

Elara’s grip tightens on the datapad. Her knuckles are white. She glances at her wrist. The internal clock on her interface blinks: 04:12 AM.

ELARA
I need ten minutes. That’s all. Ten minutes and I’m out of your hair.

SILAS
It is four-twelve and nine seconds. You were scheduled for deletion at four-ten.

He steps forward. The floorboards do not creak under his weight. 

ELARA
Vance sent you to clean up, right? I can make it worth your while. I have credits.

SILAS
I do not require currency.

Silas’s thumb taps against the grip of his pistol. Tap. Tap. Tap. A rigid, compulsive rhythm.

ELARA
Everyone requires something.

SILAS
I require the signal to stop.

Elara watches his thumb. She begins to hum. A low, steady melody. She locks the notes perfectly into the tempo of his tapping.

Silas freezes. His thumb stops. 

SILAS
Where did you hear that tempo?

ELARA
It helps, doesn't it? When the noise gets too loud. An anchor.

Silas lowers the gun a fraction of an inch. 

SILAS
Stop making that sound.

ELARA
(hums louder, keeping the beat)

She slowly reaches for a heavy, analog copper COIL on the desk beside her.

SILAS
I said stop.

ELARA
How long does it take for a bullet to reach me?

SILAS
From this distance, zero-point-zero-two seconds.

ELARA
Then you better be fast.

She lunges. 

Silas’s finger tightens on the trigger. 

CUT TO:
working.65:
INT. PIRATE BROADCAST ROOM / ELARA'S MIND - CONTINUOUS

SILAS levels his suppressed pistol. His finger tightens on the trigger. His eyes remain fixed, unblinking.

The air between them ripples. 

The grime-streaked walls of the broadcast room bleach into a sterile, infinite white. The monitors, the cables, and Knox’s frantic face dissolve into a featureless void.

SILAS freezes. He is a marble statue in a sea of milk.

A shadow falls over Elara. It is miles high.

JULIAN VANCE towers above her, a digital projection rendered in soft, therapeutic light. He looks down with the weary patience of a gardener tending to a blight.

VANCE
You’re over-encumbered, Elara. The bandwidth required to host this... it’s beyond your current specs.

Elara tries to stand. Her legs are lead. She collapses to her knees, palms slapping against the invisible floor.

ELARA
(straining)
How... long?

VANCE
The purge is already at sixty percent. We’re just clearing the cache. Think of it as a necessary tax for the evolution of the species.

Inside her skull, a high-pitched whine ascends. It sounds like a server farm cooling fan spinning until the bearings shatter.

A thick ribbon of dark, syrupy blood leaks from Elara’s right nostril. It drips onto the white floor, the only speck of color in the void.

VANCE
Your father’s face. The smell of your mother’s kitchen. They’re just redundant files now. Fragments taking up valuable real estate.

Elara’s eyes roll back. 

FLASHES: A rusted swing set. A hand-written medical bill marked OVERDUE. The texture of a worn blanket.

The images shred. They turn into jagged digital noise and vanish.

ELARA
(a whisper)
Stop.

VANCE
I’m optimizing you. 

Back in the PHYSICAL WORLD, Elara’s body jerks. Her head thrashes against the equipment rack. 

KNOX scrambles toward her, hands hovering over the neural interface cables.

KNOX
The signal is flatlining! Elara, if you don't hold the bridge, the upload dies with you!

Silas remains motionless, caught in the buffer. His jaw twitches. A micro-tremor shakes his gun hand.

In the WHITE VOID, Vance kneels. Even his projection seems to radiate a synthetic warmth.

VANCE
It doesn’t have to hurt. Just let the drive spin down. Silence is much more efficient than memory.

Elara’s fingers claw at the floor. She starts to hum. A soft, cracked melody. A childhood lullaby.

VANCE
That file is corrupted. Let it go.

ELARA
How... much... left?

VANCE
Of your mind? Not enough to matter.

Elara looks up. Her eyes are bloodshot, the capillaries bursting under the psychic pressure.

ELARA
The... data.

VANCE
Ninety percent erased. You’re almost clean.

Elara’s hum grows louder, drowning out the whine of the implant. She reaches into the space where her memories used to be and finds the heat. The fire of the overheating chip.

ELARA
(to herself)
Finish the... transaction.

She slams her forehead against the white floor. 

The void cracks. 

CUT TO:
working.66:
INT. SOMNUS MAINFRAME (MINDSCAPE) - CONTINUOUS

White. Infinite, blinding white. 

ELARA (20s) stands in the center of a geometric grid that stretches into a vanishing point. She is translucent, flickering like a weak signal. 

A dozen jagged, glowing shards of light—the encrypted files—hover around her. They pulse with a low, rhythmic thrum.

JULIAN VANCE (50s) walks toward her. He wears a tailored suit of pure light. He looks rested. Healthy. 

VANCE
The resistance is what hurts, Elara. You’re fighting the natural flow of the data. 

Elara’s knees buckle. She gasps, her hands clutching her head. Her fingers sink into her own skull like it’s made of soft clay.

ELARA
How long... until it’s done?

VANCE
Time is a legacy metric. We’re moving beyond it. Think of this as a system-wide optimization. We’re just reclaiming the idle cycles.

He reaches out, his hand hovering inches from her face. His left eye twitches—a rhythmic, mechanical tic.

VANCE (CONT'D)
Your grief is just noise in the signal. A legacy bug. Once we scrub the encryption, you’ll feel nothing but the synergy of the network.

ELARA
Nothing.

VANCE
Total peace. No more bills. No more debt. Just the quiet of a perfectly managed server. 

Vance begins to make a scrubbing motion with his hands, as if cleaning an invisible window. 

Elara looks at her hands. They are dissolving into strings of binary code. She looks at the glowing shards of the murder tape.

ELARA
How much... for the truth?

VANCE
(soothing)
Truth is a variable. Efficiency is a constant. 

Elara stops fighting. She lets her hands fall to her sides. 

She closes her eyes. 

A soft, melodic HUMMING starts. A childhood lullaby. It is thin and wavering.

VANCE (CONT'D)
That’s it. Lean into the bandwidth. Let the architecture support you.

Elara’s humming grows louder. It grates against the sterile hum of the grid. 

She isn't pushing the data away anymore. She is pulling it in.

The glowing shards rush toward her chest, sinking into her skin. Her translucent form turns a bruised, oily purple.

VANCE (CONT'D)
What are you doing? You’re spiking the load.

ELARA
(through gritted teeth)
I’m paying... in full.

She leans her head back and screams. It isn't a digital sound. It’s the sound of a throat tearing.

Black, jagged static erupts from her eyes. 

The pristine white floor cracks. From the fissures, a flood of dark, grainy images pours out: 

A hospital monitor flatlining. 
An empty refrigerator. 
A father’s cold hand.

The images aren't files. They are memories, raw and uncompressed.

VANCE
Stop. You’re corrupting the environment! This isn't part of the rollout!

Vance frantically realigns the air in front of him, trying to swipe the memories away. His hands come back stained with black ink.

The white architecture begins to peel like burning wallpaper. Underneath the "utopia" is a void of screaming code.

Elara steps toward him. Each footstep leaves a heavy, wet smear of shadow on the grid.

ELARA
Take it. Take all of it.

VANCE
The system can't... the capacity... 

The lullaby becomes a roar of distortion. 

The geometric grid shatters. The white walls of the mindscape explode outward, revealing the cold, dark machinery of the real-world server room behind the projection.

Vance’s avatar flickers. His suit of light is covered in digital filth. He looks at his hands and begins to scrub them with terrifying intensity.

VANCE (CONT'D)
It was supposed to be clean. It was supposed to be perfect.

ELARA
It’s just us.

Elara grabs the air. She twists it. 

The entire mainframe shudders. The sound of a billion hard drives grinding to a halt echoes through the void.

The white light dies. 

CUT TO:
working.67:
INT. PIRATE BROADCAST ROOM - CONTINUOUS

Elara’s eyelids snap back. Her pupils are pinpricks, fixed on the ceiling.

She gasps, a wet, rattling sound. The white digital haze of the Somnus void evaporates. 

Reality hits. The smell of ozone. The hum of cooling fans. 

Silas stands five feet away. Pistol leveled at her forehead. His finger tightens on the trigger. He is a statue of corporate intent.

ELARA
Now.

She lunges. Her boots skid on the metal floor. She slams her cracked datapad into an open port on Knox’s archaic console.

KNOX
The handshake isn't clearing! The encryption is too thick—

ELARA
Force it.

Elara ignores the gun. She reaches past Knox and punches a heavy, physical toggle switch. Red. Industrial.

Silas’s arm jerks. A mechanical whine emits from the base of his skull. His eyes cycle through a rapid strobe of amber light.

SILAS
Error. Internal... conflict. Directives... overlapping.

The pistol heavy in his hand, Silas tries to steady his aim. His wrist snaps downward as if pulled by an invisible wire. He stumbles back, crashing into a rack of servers. 

KNOX
The signal is out. It’s hitting every node in the city.

Knox’s hands fly across the keys, but she keeps her distance from the monitors, her body coiled in a defensive hunch.

KNOX
I’ve never seen a data burst this size. It’s... it’s beautiful. It’s a massacre.

Elara watches the progress bar on the main monitor. 88%. 92%. 

Inside the monitor’s reflection, a digital ghost of VANCE flickers. His face distorts, stretching into a pixelated mask of rage. He screams, but no sound comes out—only the high-pitched screech of a failing modem.

Vance’s image shatters into a cloud of silent, white squares. 

SILAS
(monotone)
Connection... lost. The sky is... empty.

Silas collapses to his knees. He blinks rapidly, his hands trembling against the floor tiles. The robotic stillness is gone, replaced by a human frailty.

KNOX
It’s done. The upload is global. The world just woke up to the murder files.

Elara doesn't look at the screen. She doesn't celebrate. She pulls her phone from her pocket and checks her bank balance app. The numbers are static. 

ELARA
How long until they track the bounce to this coordinates?

KNOX
Does it matter? You just set their entire infrastructure on fire. Vance is done.

Elara pulls the datapad from the console. The screen is dead, fried by the surge.

ELARA
My mother’s hospital bill is due in three hours. 

Knox stops. She looks at Elara, then at the massive broadcast array still pulsing with the outbound data. 

KNOX
The revolution doesn't have a payroll, kid. You wanted the truth. You got it.

ELARA
I wanted a way out.

Elara grabs a heavy jacket from the back of a chair. She doesn't look back at Silas, who is staring at his own palms as if seeing them for the first time.

ELARA
We have ten minutes before the cleaners arrive. Pack the drives.

KNOX
(shrinking into her coat)
I can't leave the tower.

ELARA
Then you die with the gear. 

Elara heads for the heavy steel door. She doesn't wait for an answer.

CUT TO:
working.68:
EXT. METROPOLIS - NIGHT

The analog broadcast tower. Steel cables vibrate. A low, electric thrum rattles the air. 

High above the smog, the transmitter pulses. Blue light bleeds into the clouds.

EXT. CITY STREETS - CONTINUOUS

Giant LED billboards—usually selling "Perfect Sleep"—flicker. Static tears the image. 

JULIAN VANCE appears on screen. Not the polished CEO. This is raw, hidden-camera footage. He nods. A suppressed pistol fires. A whistleblower falls. 

A COURIER stops his bike. He stares up. His eyes glow with the neural feed. He gasps, clutching his temples. 

The crowd halts. A thousand faces tilted toward the sky. 

A WOMAN rips the Somnus patch from her neck. She screams. It starts a fire.

INT. SOMNUS HQ - OFFICE - CONTINUOUS

VANCE stands by his analog garden. He prunes a white orchid with silver shears. 

On the wall, forty monitors show the same thing: his own face, committing murder. 

Vance puts the shears down. He takes a silk handkerchief from his pocket. He wipes his palms. Once. Twice. 

The heavy oak door shudders. The sound of boots on marble. 

VANCE
(to himself)
The architecture was sound. The users... the users are simply too volatile.

The glass wall behind him shatters. FEDERAL AGENTS swarm in. Red and blue strobes paint the room. 

Vance doesn't look at them. He realigns a fountain pen on his desk so it sits perfectly parallel to the edge.

EXT. PIRATE RADIO TOWER - ROOFTOP - CONTINUOUS

KNOX hunches over the console. Her fingers fly across the keys. She wraps her arms around her ribs, squeezing tight.

KNOX
Eighty-nine percent saturation. The firewalls are ash. 

JAX sits on a crate, tapping a frantic binary rhythm on his thigh. He looks at the city below. It’s glowing with a new, jagged light.

JAX
You did it. You actually burned the sky. 

KNOX
History is just a series of corrected data points.

ELARA leans against the parapet. She’s pale. Blood crusts under her nose. 

She isn't looking at the screens. She’s looking at a crumpled hospital bill in her hand. 

ELARA
How long until the servers stay down?

JAX
They're dead, Elara. The whole network is flatlining. 

Elara starts to hum. A soft, low lullaby. It’s thin and brittle.

EXT. PLAZA - CONTINUOUS

SILAS stands in the middle of a riot. Protesters sprint past him, hurling bricks through corporate glass. 

Silas doesn't move. His left eye twitches. His hands shake. 

The "Perfect Clarity" in his head is gone. A thousand voices—old, forgotten voices—flood back in. 

SILAS
Three... forty-two... AM. The colors are too loud.

He looks at his hands. They're covered in soot. He looks at a nearby wall. 

He pulls a charcoal stick from a discarded bag. He begins to draw a delicate, sweeping curve on the concrete.

EXT. METROPOLIS - NIGHT

The Somnus logo atop the tallest skyscraper flickers. The 'S' dies. Then the rest. 

The city plunges into darkness, lit only by the fires of the people waking up.

FADE OUT.
working.69:
INT. SUNLIT BEDROOM - DAY

Silence. Not the ringing void of a vacuum, but the heavy, velvet quiet of a soundproofed sanctuary. 

Dust motes drift through a column of thick, amber light. They move with an impossible slowness, dancing over a floor of polished wide-plank oak.

ELARA (24) sits on the edge of a bed. The mattress is a mountain of white linen and goose down. She looks small. Fragile.

Her boots—scuffed, salt-stained, and caked with the grey grime of the lower sectors—rest on a hand-woven silk rug. They look like an insult to the room.

Elara remains perfectly still. Her head is cocked to the side. She is listening.

She waits for the ping. That sharp, electronic needle that usually pricks the base of her skull every six seconds to signal a data packet arrival.

Nothing.

She waits for the low-frequency hum of the Somnus server-link. The sound of her own brain being leased by the millisecond. 

Only the sound of her own breathing. Slow. Shallow.

She lifts a datapad from the nightstand. The glass is flawless. No spiderweb cracks. No oily thumbprints. 

She taps the screen. The brightness doesn't hurt her eyes.

ON THE SCREEN:

A banking interface. Clean. Minimalist. 

TOTAL BALANCE: $428,000.00

Below the number, a scrolling list of transactions. All marked in green.

ST. JUDE’S INTENSIVE CARE - PAID IN FULL.
PROSTHETIC LUNG LEASE - OWNERSHIP TRANSFERRED.
ARREARS CLEARANCE - COMPLETE.

Elara stares at the word 'COMPLETE'. She traces the letters with her thumb. 

She swipes the screen. A new window opens. A high-definition video feed. 

In a room filled with soft blue light, her MOTHER sleeps. Not the grey, skeletal woman from the tenements. Her skin looks hydrated. Plump. A high-end ventilator hums with the precision of a Swiss watch.

A NURSE enters the frame, tucking a thermal blanket around the older woman’s shoulders. The nurse looks at the camera and offers a small, professional nod.

Elara’s hand drops. The datapad thuds softly onto the duvet. 

She looks at her wrist. The digital watch she’s worn since she was sixteen. 

09:42 AM.

Usually, this is the peak. The hour where the corporate servers demand the most bandwidth. The hour where her vision flickers with ghost-images of spreadsheets and encrypted code.

The sunlight on the wall stays steady. The shadows don't vibrate.

She reaches up, her fingers trembling as they find the neural port behind her ear. The plastic housing is cool to the touch. The status LED, which has pulsed a frantic, bleeding red for months, is dark.

She is offline.

The tension that has held her spine rigid for years begins to dissolve. It’s a physical collapse, starting at her jaw and working its way down to her leaden limbs. 

Her shoulders drop. Her chest expands, taking in a lungful of air that doesn't taste like ozone or exhaust.

She swings her legs up onto the bed. She doesn't even take off the boots. 

She sinks into the pillows. They swallow her.

Elara stares at the ceiling. The plaster is a perfect, unblemished white. 

A sob hitches in her throat—sharp and jagged—but she doesn't let it out. She doesn't have to cry. She doesn't have to bargain. She doesn't have to do anything.

Her eyelids grow heavy. The gold light of the room turns to a soft, hazy orange behind her lids.

She begins to hum. It’s a low, vibrating sound in her chest. A childhood lullaby, the notes slightly off-key but steady. 

The humming slows. Trails off into a sigh.

Her fingers, curled into the bedsheets, slowly relax. They go limp.

The world narrows down to the warmth of the sun on her skin. 

Elara closes her eyes.

She falls. Not into a digital grid or a corporate nightmare.

Just sleep. 

Deep. Dark. Empty.

FADE OUT.
working.70:
INT. PIRATE BROADCAST ROOM - NIGHT

Golden sunlight washes over ELARA (20s). She stands on a balcony overlooking a city of ivory and glass. The air smells of jasmine.

She presses a finger to a floating glass display. A progress bar hits 100%.

ELARA
(A breath)
It’s over.

The sun STUTTERS. A jagged black line slices through the sky.

The golden light PEELS away like burning paper. The ivory towers dissolve into RUST and CORRUGATED TIN.

The jasmine scent is replaced by the STENCH of ozone and stale sweat.

Elara is not on a balcony. She is slumped in a plastic chair in a room of shadows.

Her head rests against the console. A thick, dark line of blood has dried on her chin. 

Her eyes are wide. Fixed. They do not blink as the fluorescent light overhead HUMS.

The monitor in front of her displays a single, static-filled prompt: DATA PURGED. 0% UPLOADED.

SILAS (40s) steps from the corner. He moves with a mechanical, unwavering stillness. 

He stands over Elara. He reaches out and lifts her chin. Her head lolls to the side, lifeless.

Silas taps a small transmitter at his temple.

SILAS
The drive is scrubbed. The transmission was blocked at the source.

INTERCUT WITH:

INT. VANCE’S OFFICE - NIGHT

JULIAN VANCE (50s) stands in his carefully pruned analog garden. He uses a silver pair of shears to snip a dead leaf from a bonsai tree.

VANCE
(Soothing)
And the host? Is there any residual bandwidth?

INT. PIRATE BROADCAST ROOM - NIGHT

Silas checks the display on a handheld tablet. A flat line crawls across the screen.

SILAS
Zero activity. The neural architecture has collapsed. 

VANCE (V.O.)
A necessary optimization. We cannot allow corrupted files to compromise the global rollout.

Silas looks at the blood on Elara’s face. He pulls a white handkerchief from his suit pocket.

He does not wipe her face. Instead, he carefully cleans a spot of dust from the console’s keyboard.

SILAS
The site is secure. 

VANCE (V.O.)
Excellent. Reclaim the hardware and return. We move to the next phase in ten minutes.

Silas turns. He walks toward the exit, his footsteps echoing on the metal floor.

Elara remains frozen. A human shell trapped in the dark.

Beyond the window, the city’s massive neon grid pulses.

FADE OUT.

snapshot.title: The Somnus Drive
